title: castilleja talamancensis
intro: Castilleja talamancensis, also known as Mountain Indian paintbrush, is a perennial wildflower native to Central America, particularly in the mountainous regions of Costa Rica and Panama. The plant is known for its distinctive red and yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Castilleja talamancensis include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
